task_summary: SP23a landed a deterministic CLI-only mew-wisp speech bubble by staging mew-authored source and test patches, working around verifier rollback by using dry-run approvals with deferred verification.

tests_run.command: MEW_GHOST_TERMINAL_WIDTH=96 UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format human --form cat --watch-count 1 --interval 0
tests_run.result: rendered centered cat, ASCII speech bubble, and resident HUD in terminal human output

notes: Codex did not write product code. The operator steered mew into staged dry-run patches and approved them with deferred verification to avoid losing useful near-miss patches. The final speech bubble appears before the resident HUD in both default and cat human output, normalizes non-ASCII text, wraps long words, and accounts for cat-surface left padding so bubble lines do not exceed the configured terminal width. Existing state, HTML, watch, live-desk, launcher, details gating, cat mask similarity, and resident panel tests remained covered by the focused test file.
